  -- Using symmetry and 1/‖ρ‖ < 1/ρ.im, we have sum ... ≤ Sigma ...
  have bound_sigma : riemannZeta.zeroes_sum (Set.Ioo 0 1) (Set.Ioo (-T) T) (fun ρ ↦ ‖x^(ρ - 1) / ρ‖) ≤ 
      ε₂ I x 0.9 T + ε₃ I x 0.9 σ₂ N T + ε₄ I t₀ x σ₂ K T := by
    -- This step would combine Propositions 3.6, 3.8, and Corollary 3.12
    -- splitting the range of Sigma into [0, 0.9], [0.9, σ₂], [σ₂, 1].
    sorry

  have bound_x : Eψ x ≤ ε₁ x T + ε₂ I x 0.9 T + ε₃ I x 0.9 σ₂ N T + ε₄ I t₀ x σ₂ K T := by
    rw [ε₁]
    apply le_trans bound1
    apply add_le_add_right bound_sigma
    
  -- 4. Monotonicity: ε(x) ≤ ε(x₀)
  -- Each term ε₁, ε₂, ε₃, ε₄ is decreasing for x large enough (log x > 1000 is sufficient).
  -- ε₄ is handled by Proposition 3.14.
  have mono : ε₁ x T + ε₂ I x 0.9 T + ε₃ I x 0.9 σ₂ N T + ε₄ I t₀ x σ₂ K T ≤ ε I x₀ σ₂ c N K := by sorry
  
  exact le_trans bound_x mono
